sections.title: 1. Concept Overview
sections.body:
### Polynomials Class 10: Zeroes, Division Algorithm & Relationship Between Zeroes

Polynomials Class 10: Zeroes, Division Algorithm & Relationship Between Zeroes
Your complete guide to zeroes, sum-product relationships, division algorithm, and forming polynomials from zeroes!
Why Polynomials Matters More Than You Think
Polynomials (NCERT Chapter 2) might seem like a short chapter, but it's foundational for your entire algebra preparation. It typically carries 3-5 marks in the CBSE board exam, and more importantly, the concepts here directly connect to Quadratic Equations (Chapter 4), which carries even more marks.
The chapter focuses on three big ideas:
1. Zeroes of a polynomial — the values of  where the polynomial equals zero.
2. Relationship between zeroes and coefficients — elegant formulas connecting the sum and product of zeroes to the coefficients.
3. Division algorithm — dividing one polynomial by another, just like long division with numbers.
Master these three, and you'll breeze through both this chapter and Quadratic Equations. Let's dive in!
Types of Polynomials: Quick Recap
A polynomial in is an expression of the form , where are real numbers and is a non-negative int…
**Crawled sources (internet):**
- [Polynomials Class 10: Zeroes, Division Algorithm & Relationship Between Zeroes](https://www.sparkedmaths.com/blog/cbse-class-10-polynomials-guide)

sections.title: 2. Key Definitions
sections.body:
**Definitions extracted from crawled pages:**

- A polynomial in is an expression of the form , where are real numbers and is a non-negative integer.
- The degree of a polynomial is the highest power of with a non-zero coefficient.
- The zeroes of a polynomial are the -coordinates of the points where the graph of intersects the -axis.
- Linear polynomial : The graph is a straight line. It intersects the -axis at exactly one point , which is the only zero.
- Quadratic polynomial : The graph is a parabola. It can intersect the -axis at:
- This is the most important section of the chapter and the most frequently tested concept in board exams.
- where is any non-zero real number (usually taken as 1 for simplicity).
- Problem: If one zero of is the reciprocal of the other, find the value of .

sections.title: 3. Core Concepts
sections.body:
### Polynomials Class 10: Zeroes, Division Algorithm & Relationship Between Zeroes

Polynomials Class 10: Zeroes, Division Algorithm & Relationship Between Zeroes
Your complete guide to zeroes, sum-product relationships, division algorithm, and forming polynomials from zeroes!
Why Polynomials Matters More Than You Think
Polynomials (NCERT Chapter 2) might seem like a short chapter, but it's foundational for your entire algebra preparation. It typically carries 3-5 marks in the CBSE board exam, and more importantly, the concepts here directly connect to Quadratic Equations (Chapter 4), which carries even more marks.
The chapter focuses on three big ideas:
1. Zeroes of a polynomial — the values of  where the polynomial equals zero.
2. Relationship between zeroes and coefficients — elegant formulas connecting the sum and product of zeroes to the coefficients.
3. Division algorithm — dividing one polynomial by another, just like long division with numbers.
Master these three, and you'll breeze through both this chapter and Quadratic Equations. Let's dive in!
Types of Polynomials: Quick Recap
A polynomial in is an expression of the form , where are real numbers and is a non-negative integer.
The degree of a polynomial is the highest power of with a non-zero coefficient.
| Degree | Name | General Form | Max Zeroes | 
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Constant | (where ) | 0 | 
| 1 | Linear | 1 | |
| 2 | Quadratic | 2 | |
| 3 | Cubic | 3 | 
Important: A polynomial of degree has at most zeroes. It could have fewer (some might be complex numbers, which you'll learn in higher classes), but never more than .
Geometrical Meaning of Zeroes
The zeroes of a polynomial are the -coordinates of the points where the graph of intersects the -axis.
Linear polynomial : The graph is a straight line. It intersects the -axis at exactly one point , which is the only zero.
Quadratic polynomial : The graph is a parabola. It can intersect the -axis at:
- Two points — two distinct real zeroes (discriminan…

sections.title: 6. Common Mistakes
sections.body:
**Common pitfalls mentioned:**

- 1. Sign Error in Sum of Zeroes:
- Mistake: Writing instead of .
- Mistake: Thinking the zeroes are the coefficients , , .
- Mistake: Subtracting incorrectly during polynomial long division, especially with negative signs.
- Mistake: Claiming the polynomial is unique when only given the zeroes.
- Mistake: Assuming a degree- polynomial always has real zeroes.

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Polynomials**._

**Practice questions from crawled sources:**

1. 1. Zeroes of a polynomial — the values of where the polynomial equals zero.
2. 2. Relationship between zeroes and coefficients — elegant formulas connecting the sum and product of zeroes to the coefficients.
3. 3. Division algorithm — dividing one polynomial by another, just like long division with numbers.
4. 1. Arrange both polynomials in descending order of degree.
5. 2. Divide the leading term of the dividend by the leading term of the divisor. This is the first term of the quotient.
6. 3. Multiply the divisor by this term and subtract from the dividend.
7. 4. The result is the new dividend. Repeat from step 2.
8. 5. Continue until the degree of the remainder is less than the degree of the divisor.
9. 1. Sign Error in Sum of Zeroes:
10. 2. Confusing Zeroes with Coefficients:

**Additional syllabus-based set** _(auto-generated for review — Phase-II ingest)_

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Zeros of a polynomial** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Polynomials.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Zeros of a polynomial** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Relationship between zeros and coefficients** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Polynomials.

sections.title: 11. Video Transcript Summary
sections.body:
_Teacher recap — what a strong Class 10 video lesson on **Polynomials** should cover:_

**Lesson flow (as if narrated on screen):**

1. **Hook** — Start with a daily-life example tied to Polynomials, not a textbook definition.
2. **Zeros of a polynomial** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
3. **Relationship between zeros and coefficients** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
4. **Division algorithm for polynomials** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
5. **Formulas** — Write For ax^2 + bx + c: sum of zeros = -b/a on the board, explain each symbol, then solve one board-style numerical.
6. **Common mistake** — Warn about the error students make most often in exams.
7. **Recap** — Three bullet points to remember before moving to practice questions.

**After watching, you should be able to:**

- Explain Zeros of a polynomial in everyday language.
- Draw or describe one diagram related to Polynomials.
- Attempt at least two short-answer questions without notes.

**Videos linked for this chapter:**

- 🔍 [CBSE class 10 Polynomials NCERT explained Hindi English](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=CBSE+class+10+Polynomials+NCERT+explained+Hindi+English)
- 🔍 [Polynomials class 10 one shot revision](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Polynomials+class+10+one+shot+revision)
- 🔍 [NCERT Mathematics class 10 Polynomials animation](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=NCERT+Mathematics+class+10+Polynomials+animation)
